preview_text:
The model rates France as a heavy favorite with an 84% win probability, leaving Iraq with less than 1% chance of victory. The gap reflects a substantial disparity in the model's assessment of the two sides' relative strength.

A draw registers at 16% in the model forecast, making it the only plausible alternative to a France victory. The win-loss spread is wide; Iraq's path to three points is remote, and even a draw would constitute a strong result for the underdog. France is expected to control the match decisively.

The market consensus diverges from the model on two counts. Sportsbooks assign France 88% and draws only 9%, implying greater confidence in a France win and lower belief in a stalemate. Polymarket prices lean even further toward France at 90%, with draws at 7%. Both sources rate Iraq's upset chances at roughly 3%, substantially above the model's 1%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better reflects ground truth.
